items.markdown:
![Lead Qualification: The Secret to Getting Qualified Leads](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/06041601-1876-0139-0000-03d3b0cd49b7:4)
Lead qualification is `the process of evaluating potential buyers to determine if they are a good fit for your product or service` . Its primary goal is to filter out prospects who are unlikely to buy, allowing sales teams to focus their time and resources on opportunities most likely to close.[](https://www.salesforce.com/blog/sales/lead-qualification/) [[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/blog/sales/lead-qualification/)[[2]](https://www.11x.ai/blog/what-is-lead-qualification-and-how-to-scale-it)

A strong qualification process saves time, shortens sales cycles, and improves overall win rates. It is typically divided into two main stages:[](https://salesmotion.io/blog/what-is-lead-qualification) [[1]](https://salesmotion.io/blog/what-is-lead-qualification)

1. Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs)

These are leads that have engaged with your marketing efforts (e.g., downloaded an ebook, attended a webinar, or frequently visited your website). They have shown interest but may not yet be ready to make a purchase.[](https://www.highspot.com/en-gb/blog/lead-qualification/) [[1]](https://www.highspot.com/en-gb/blog/lead-qualification/)[[2]](https://www.tableau.com/learn/articles/marketing-qualified-lead)[[3]](https://www.bigcontacts.com/blog/lead-qualification/)[[4]](https://www.leadshook.com/blog/lead-qualification/)[[5]](https://www.revenue.io/inside-sales-glossary/what-is-a-marketing-qualified-lead-mql)

2. Sales Qualified Leads (SQLs)

These are prospects that the sales team has vetted and confirmed as ready to enter the active sales pipeline . They have a proven need for your solution and the ability to buy.[](https://www.11x.ai/blog/what-is-lead-qualification-and-how-to-scale-it) [[1]](https://www.11x.ai/blog/what-is-lead-qualification-and-how-to-scale-it)[[2]](https://jobs.gartner.com/jobs/job/93948-lead-qualification-specialist-irving-tx-or-fort-myers-fl/)[[3]](https://www.highspot.com/en-gb/blog/lead-qualification/)[[4]](https://croclub.com/marketing/what-is-a-lead-in-marketing/)[[5]](https://www.pipedrive.com/en/blog/lead-qualification)

Common Qualification Frameworks To determine if a lead is an SQL, sales professionals commonly use one of the following evaluation frameworks:

- **BANT (Budget, Authority, Need, Time):** Assesses whether the lead has the funds to pay, the decision-making power to make the purchase, a genuine problem your product solves, and a clear timeline for buying.
- **MEDDIC (Metrics, Economic buyer, Decision criteria, Decision process, Identify pain, Champion):** A highly detailed, B2B-focused framework used to map out exactly who makes the buying decisions and what criteria they use.
- **CHAMP (Challenges, Authority, Money, Prioritization):** Centers the conversation first on the prospect's pain points and challenges, making the solution feel more tailored.[](https://s2wmedia.com/blog/lead-qualification-process-a-guide-to-qualifying-and-converting-leads) [[1]](https://s2wmedia.com/blog/lead-qualification-process-a-guide-to-qualifying-and-converting-leads)[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/shorts/IM4eca8JpEg)[[3]](https://outplay.ai/blog/lead-qualification)[[4]](https://www.socoselling.com/lead-qualification-training/)[[5]](https://www.pipedrive.com/en/blog/lead-qualification)

For more information on setting up these frameworks for your business, you can browse guides on platforms like [Salesforce](https://www.salesforce.com/blog/sales/lead-qualification/) or [Pipedrive](https://www.pipedrive.com/en/blog/lead-qualification).

items.title: What is the meaning of lead qualification?

expanded_element.title: Master Lead Qualification: The Ultimate Guide | Freshsales
expanded_element.description: Lead qualification is defined as the process of determining whether a potential prospect aligns with the Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) and, consequently, gauging their likelihood of purchasing a product or service.

expanded_element.title: What is lead qualification and why does it matter?
expanded_element.description: Once a lead has been qualified, the next step is to nurture it —or get in contact and understand the prospect's needs and wants before bringing them further along in the sales pipeline. A regular cadence for follow-ups. Follow-ups are a great and easy way to nurture a qualified lead.

expanded_element.featured_title: 6 ways to generate more qualified leads

expanded_element.description:

Map your buyer's journey. ...
Develop a B2B content marketing strategy. ...
Optimize your website. ...
Align sales and marketing. ...
Continuously test, measure, and refine.

items.title: What is an example of a qualified lead?

expanded_element.title: What Is A Marketing Qualified Lead (MQL)? - Tableau
expanded_element.description:
Examples of Marketing Qualified Lead actions:
 Submitting an email address for a newsletter or mailing list. Favoriting items or adding items to a wishlist. Adding items to the shopping cart. Repeating site visits or spending a lot of time on your site.
